title: Strands Robots connects Hugging Face LeRobot workflows to agent-controlled hardware
friendlyTitle: A new workflow lets agents control robot simulations and hardware
summary: A Hugging Face post from Amazon contributors explains Strands Robots, an Apache-2.0 orchestration layer that exposes LeRobot-compatible robots as tools for Strands agents. The workflow can record demonstrations, produce LeRobotDatasets, run policies in simulation, and switch similar code to real robot hardware.
friendlySummary: Amazon contributors showed how Strands Robots can connect AI agents with Hugging Face LeRobot tools. Developers can start in simulation and later move similar code to real robot arms.
whyItMatters: Robotics is becoming more accessible when datasets, simulation, policies, and physical devices share one workflow. For builders, this lowers the barrier to testing robot-agent ideas before buying or touching hardware.
friendlyWhyItMatters: This makes robotics experiments easier to start, because teams can practice in software before working with real machines.
